does: This work describes an implementation of agentic commerce capabilities within ChatGPT, introducing a protocol-driven architecture that transforms product discovery from text-based search into an interactive, visually rich experience. The system enables users to browse products, view side-by-side comparisons, and seamlessly integrate merchant systems through a standardized protocol. The approach leverages ChatGPT's conversational interface and reasoning capabilities to interpret user preferences and intents, translating them into structured product queries and personalized recommendations.
connects: This builds on prior work in conversational AI for e-commerce, extending beyond simple retrieval systems by incorporating multi-modal interaction combining text and rich visual content. It responds to limitations in existing product discovery paradigms where large language models struggle to interface directly with merchant databases and inventory systems. The work draws on agent-based architectures in language models, protocol-driven integrations seen in API ecosystems, and research on improving LLM grounding through external tool use and structured outputs.
enables: This foundation opens pathways for more sophisticated agentic commerce systems where language models can directly negotiate with merchant platforms, manage transactions, and provide genuinely real-time inventory information. It positions future work on personalized shopping agents that can learn user preferences over time, cross-platform price comparison at scale, and merchant APIs optimized for AI-driven discovery. The protocol framework could expand to support dynamic pricing negotiations, subscription services, and integration with supply chain systems, while the visual interaction model establishes patterns for multimodal agent interfaces beyond commerce.
